name: Agastache 'Apricot Sprite'
description: A compact agastache with peachy-apricot flowers and aromatic foliage. Excellent for containers and small garden spaces while maintaining the hummingbird-attracting habit.

name: Agastache 'Firebird'
description: A warm red-coral agastache with aromatic foliage and extended summer-to-fall bloom. Heat and drought tolerant, prized for attracting hummingbirds and pollinators.

name: Annabelle
description: A deciduous shrub with large, pure white flower panicles from early summer through fall. Extremely popular for its abundant, long-lasting blooms and easy care.

name: Autumn Damask Rose
description: A repeat-blooming form of the Damask rose, one of the oldest roses known to rebloom, producing fragrant deep-pink flowers in early summer and again in autumn. Also known as the 'Four Seasons Rose' or 'Rose of Castile'.

name: Autumn Sun Coneflower
description: A tall perennial rudbeckia with bright golden-yellow flowers and a greenish-yellow center, blooming late into autumn. Excellent for cutting and back-of-border planting.

name: Bathsheba Rose
description: Bathsheba is a David Austin climbing rose with large, full rosettes in soft apricot-yellow with a paler reverse and a strong myrrh fragrance. Repeat-flowering and disease-resistant, good for walls, obelisks and mixed borders.

name: Black Adder Agastache
description: Black Adder is a dwarf agastache with deep purple-burgundy flower spikes and very aromatic foliage. It is compact and tidy, ideal for small spaces while attracting hummingbirds and butterflies.

name: Blue Fortune Agastache
description: A popular hybrid agastache producing tall spikes of lavender-blue flowers from mid-summer to autumn. Attracts butterflies and hummingbirds, with aromatic foliage.

name: Blue Girl Rose
description: A hybrid tea rose producing fragrant, lavender-mauve blooms on long stems ideal for cutting. Moderately vigorous with good disease resistance and continuous flowering from late spring through fall.

name: Blue Allium
description: A mid-height ornamental onion with delicate blue flower spheres that bloom in early summer. Excellent for adding cool tones to borders.

name: Bolero Rose
description: A Meilland floribunda (sold as Bolero) with very full, creamy-white blooms flushed pale pink at the centre and a fresh, fruity fragrance. Repeat-flowering and disease-resistant, well suited to borders and cottage gardens.

name: Brunner 'Jack Frost'
description: A striking cultivar with silvery-frosted foliage and blue forget-me-not-like flowers in spring. The metallic leaf markings brighten shaded areas year-round.

name: Brunner 'Silver Heart'
description: A variegated cultivar with silvery-white heart-shaped leaves and blue spring flowers. Provides excellent foliage interest in shade gardens.

name: California Flannel Bush 'Ken Taylor'
description: A compact, spreading Fremontodendron cultivar that grows wider than tall, making it excellent as a large-scale groundcover or bank stabiliser. Bears the same showy golden-orange flowers as the species but in a more manageable, low-arching form.

name: California Flannel Bush 'Pacific Sunset'
description: A vigorous hybrid Fremontodendron cultivar producing an exceptionally long display of large, deep golden-orange flowers from spring into summer. One of the most floriferous selections, ideal for dry slopes and walls in mild climates.

name: California Fuchsia
description: A spreading, drought-tolerant California native bearing masses of tubular scarlet-orange flowers from midsummer into autumn, beloved by hummingbirds. Formerly known as Zauschneria californica; thrives in hot, dry, sunny spots.

name: California Flannelbush
description: A fast-growing California native evergreen shrub or small tree covered in large, brilliant golden-yellow saucer-shaped flowers in spring and early summer. Extremely drought tolerant once established; dislikes summer irrigation and heavy clay soils.

name: California Lilac
description: One of the most popular California lilac cultivars, smothered in rich cobalt-blue flower clusters in spring against glossy dark-green evergreen foliage. Highly drought tolerant once established and excellent for wildlife.

name: California Poppy (Cream/White)
description: A pale cream to white-flowered form of the California poppy, offering a softer, moonlit effect in wildflower plantings. Shares the same drought tolerance and self-seeding habit as the species.

name: California Poppy (Rose/Pink Mix)
description: A deep carmine-pink cultivar of the California poppy, adding warm rosy tones to wildflower borders and gravel gardens. Equally drought tolerant and free-seeding as the classic orange species.

name: Common Orange Daylily
description: The classic tawny orange daylily, widely naturalised across roadsides and gardens, bearing bold orange trumpet flowers in early summer. Extremely vigorous and drought-tolerant, it spreads readily and is ideal for naturalising on banks or difficult spots.

name: Dames de Chenonceau Rose
description: A Delbard shrub rose bearing large, full, soft-pink blooms with a pleasant fragrance. Repeat-flowering from late spring through autumn with good disease resistance.

name: Dorothy Perkins Rose
description: A classic rambling rose introduced in 1901, producing large clusters of small, double, rose-pink blooms once in midsummer. It is vigorous and ideal for covering pergolas, fences, and arches.

name: Dublin Bay Rose
description: A popular climbing rose bearing clusters of deep crimson-red, double blooms with a light fragrance throughout summer and into autumn. It is a strong grower well suited to walls, fences, and pillars.

name: Duchess of Cornwall Rose
description: A hybrid tea rose producing large, high-centred blooms in warm peach-pink tones with a sweet fragrance. It is a vigorous, repeat-flowering variety with excellent disease resistance.

name: Ebb Tide Rose
description: Ebb Tide is a compact shrub rose producing fragrant, deep purple-red blooms from spring through fall. It offers excellent disease resistance and is ideal for small gardens, containers, and landscape borders.

name: Big Sky Sunset Coneflower
description: A Big Sky series coneflower with large, fragrant, warm-orange flowers over brown-tinted central cones. Upright and sturdy, blooming mid to late summer; drought-tolerant and loved by butterflies.

name: Giant Hyssop 'Ginger'
description: A fragrant perennial with soft peach-orange flower spikes that bloom mid-summer through fall. Attracts hummingbirds and butterflies and tolerates dry conditions once established.

name: Giant Sunflower
description: A classic giant sunflower reaching 8 feet or more with enormous golden-yellow flower heads up to 12 inches across. A bold statement plant that attracts pollinators and birds.

name: Hollyhock 'Blacknight'
description: A deep crimson hollyhock with wine-dark flowers that stands out as a landscape feature. This biennial or short-lived perennial brings bold color and vertical accent to borders.

name: Hollyhock 'Chater's Double'
description: A fully double-flowered hollyhock with ruffled petals in soft pink tones, creating a peony-like bloom. The layered blooms make this cultivar particularly ornamental.

name: Hollyhock 'Peach'
description: A warm-toned hollyhock variety with peachy-apricot blooms that add soft color to the garden. This biennial or short-lived perennial is easy to grow and beloved in traditional cottage gardens.

name: Lime Tree
description: A tropical citrus tree with fragrant white flowers followed by small, green limes. Grown for both ornamental white blooms and edible fruit in warm climates.

name: Louise Clements Rose
description: A modern shrub rose (Heirloom Roses, 1996) with fully double, old-fashioned blooms in an intense coppery-orange to apricot and a warm fragrance. Bronze-green foliage, upright habit, repeat-flowering and disease-resistant.

name: Madame Anisette Rose
description: Madame Anisette is a modern shrub rose (Tantau) with full, apricot-cream to soft-blush blooms and a strong anise, liquorice and myrrh fragrance. Repeat-flowering with good disease resistance.

name: Japanese Maple 'Miyako'
description: Miyako is a compact Japanese maple cultivar prized for its fine, deeply-lobed red foliage that deepens in autumn. It grows slowly into a graceful, spreading shrub suitable for woodland gardens and containers.

name: Miyako Rose
description: A compact Japanese shrub rose bearing clusters of fragrant, pink flowers from late spring through fall. Hardy and disease-resistant, it thrives in full sun with moderate water and is valued for its graceful form and long bloom season.

name: New England Aster
description: A tall native perennial with purple daisy-like flowers blooming in late summer and fall. Attracts butterflies and bees, and thrives in full sun with moderate moisture.

name: New England Aster 'Barr's Pink'
description: A soft pink cultivar of New England Aster with a more compact habit than the species. Blooms prolifically in late summer and fall, attracting butterflies and bees.

name: New England Aster 'Harrington's Pink'
description: A soft pink cultivar of New England Aster with delicate daisy-like flowers blooming in late summer and fall. Tall and airy, perfect for the back of borders.

name: Orange Coneflower
description: A vibrant cultivar with warm orange-gold petals and a prominent central cone. Blooms prolifically and attracts pollinators throughout the growing season.

name: Pink Damask Daylily
description: A popular daylily producing warm salmon-pink flowers with a lighter midrib over a long summer season. Vigorous and easy to grow, it is a staple of the cottage-style border.

name: Princess Charlene de Monaco Rose
description: A Meilland hybrid tea, named for Princess Charlene of Monaco, with large, very full blooms in soft apricot-pink and a strong fruity fragrance. Repeat-flowering with good disease resistance.

name: Rosa damascena (Damask Rose)
description: A classic old garden rose bearing clusters of intensely fragrant, semi-double to double soft-pink blooms on arching, thorny canes. Prized for centuries for its perfume and used commercially in the production of rose oil (attar of roses).

name: Duchesse de Verneuil (Moss Rose)
description: A classic moss rose with heavily mossed buds that open to soft pink, quartered blooms with a rich fragrance. It is a once-flowering old garden rose that makes a graceful, arching shrub in summer borders.

name: Rosa de Meaux (Centifolia Rose)
description: A miniature centifolia rose dating to the 18th century, bearing tiny, perfectly formed, pompon-style blooms in soft rose-pink with a sweet fragrance. It forms a neat, twiggy bush ideal for cottage gardens and borders.

name: Rosa de Rescht (Damask Rose)
description: A compact, bushy Damask/Portland rose producing densely petalled, deep fuchsia-crimson blooms with a strong, classic rose fragrance. It repeat-blooms reliably through summer and autumn, making it one of the best old roses for small gardens.

name: Rosa 'Fantin-Latour'
description: A centifolia rose with quartered, pale pink flowers and a strong fragrance, blooming once in early summer. An heirloom variety prized for its romantic, full blooms.

name: Rosa 'Felicia'
description: A hybrid musk rose with soft pink, fragrant flowers that bloom repeatedly throughout the season. Vigorous and disease-resistant with a bushy, spreading habit.

name: Rosa 'Fragrant Cloud'
description: A hybrid tea rose with vibrant orange-coral blooms and exceptional fragrance, flowering repeatedly from late spring to autumn. Compact and upright with excellent disease resistance.

name: Rosa 'Frau Karl Druschki'
description: A hybrid perpetual rose with large, pure white, double flowers and a strong fragrance, blooming repeatedly throughout the season. Vigorous and hardy with excellent garden presence.

name: Rosa 'Ispahan'
description: A well-known Damask rose cultivar producing an exceptionally long flush of large, loosely double, warm-pink blooms with a rich, classic rose fragrance. One of the most reliable and long-flowering of the old Damask roses.

name: Rosa 'Madame Hardy'
description: A celebrated white Damask rose bearing perfectly quartered, pure white blooms with a distinctive green button eye and a strong, fresh fragrance. Considered one of the finest white old garden roses ever bred.

name: Patience Rose
description: A David Austin English rose with full, ivory to buttermilk-white rosettes that fade almost to white, carrying a strong Old Rose and myrrh fragrance. Repeat-flowering with good disease resistance.

name: Shropshire Lad Rose
description: An English shrub rose with semi-double, cupped blooms in soft pink and a strong, sweet fragrance. Vigorous grower that flowers repeatedly.

name: Rosa 'Trigintipetala'
description: The famous 'Kazanlik' or Bulgarian rose, grown commercially in the Rose Valley of Bulgaria for the production of attar of roses. It bears loosely double, vivid pink blooms of extraordinary fragrance on vigorous, arching canes.

name: Roselle (Rosa de Jamaica)
description: Known as 'Rosa de Jamaica' in Spanish-speaking countries, this tropical annual is grown for its fleshy, deep crimson calyces used to make herbal teas and drinks. It also bears attractive pale yellow flowers with a dark centre.

name: Sally Holmes
description: A vigorous shrub rose with clusters of creamy-white, single flowers and glossy foliage. Highly disease-resistant and reliable, flowering repeatedly throughout the season.

name: Scarlet Fire
description: A tall shrub rose with brilliant scarlet-red single flowers followed by decorative orange hips. Once-flowering but spectacular in bloom and valued for its ornamental fruit.

name: Stafford Daylily
description: A striking daylily with deep crimson-red flowers marked with a yellow midrib, blooming in early to midsummer. A long-established favourite for adding bold colour to the herbaceous border.

name: Stanwell Perpetual
description: A hardy, compact shrub rose with pale pink, double flowers and a strong fragrance. Flowers continuously from spring to autumn and tolerates poor soil and cold climates.

name: Sunrise Agastache
description: Sunrise is an agastache cultivar with warm apricot-orange flower spikes and bright green foliage. It blooms reliably from early summer through fall and is heat and drought tolerant.

name: Sunset Hyssop
description: A compact agastache with vibrant orange-red flower spikes that bloom from early summer through fall. Highly fragrant and a reliable pollinator magnet in sunny gardens.

name: White Limelight Hydrangea
description: A deciduous shrub with large, cone-shaped flower panicles that open creamy-white and fade to pink in autumn. Extremely popular for its long bloom season and reliable performance in most climates.

name: White Allium
description: A compact ornamental onion with broad grey-green leaves and flat white flower heads in spring. Very drought-tolerant and excellent for rock gardens.

name: White Variegated Privet
description: A semi-evergreen shrub with lime-green and white variegated foliage and small, fragrant white flowers. Commonly used for hedging and ornamental screening.
